cards.title: Advanced Yoga Therapeutics — Principles
cards._legacyHtml:
Yoga Chikitsa (Yoga Therapy)
Yoga therapy is the individualised application of yoga practices — asana, pranayama, meditation, yoga nidra, shatkarmas, mudra, and bandha — as therapeutic interventions for specific health conditions. It is distinct from yoga exercise classes. Evidence base: over 1,200 RCTs in PubMed support yoga for specific clinical conditions. Strongest evidence: T2DM, hypertension, LBP, anxiety, depression, cardiovascular risk reduction, insomnia, COPD.
Yoga ComponentMechanismEvidence-Based IndicationContraindication
Asana (postures)Musculoskeletal alignment, proprioceptive input, circulation, parasympathetic activationLBP ★★★, musculoskeletal conditions, metabolic syndrome, cardiovascular riskAcute injury at target joint, unstable spine (spondylolisthesis >grade 2), severe osteoporosis
Pranayama (breath control)HRV ↑ (Nadi Shodhana), CO₂ sensitivity (Bhramari), sympathetic modulation, nitric oxide production (humming)Hypertension ★★★, anxiety ★★★, asthma ★★, COPD ★★, insomnia, cardiac arrhythmiaSkull fracture (Kapalabhati contraindicated). Retinal detachment (Bhastrika). Pregnancy (forceful breathing). Uncontrolled epilepsy.
Meditation / MindfulnessPFC-amygdala connectivity ↑, cortisol ↓, BDNF ↑, telomerase activation, neuroplasticityDepression ★★★, anxiety ★★★, chronic pain ★★, PTSD ★★, cognitive decline (prevention) ★★Acute psychosis (mindfulness may worsen). Trauma history: trauma-sensitive approach mandatory.
Yoga Nidra (yogic sleep)Theta brainwave induction, hippocampal consolidation, SNS → PNS shift, cortisol ↓, dopamine ↑ 65% (research)Insomnia ★★★, PTSD ★★★, chronic pain ★★, burnout, exam stress, post-surgery recoverySevere depersonalisation disorder (may worsen dissociation). Monitor carefully in psychosis history.
Shatkarmas (Kriyas)Mucosal cleansing, digestive activation, doshic balanceJala neti: chronic sinusitis ★★★, allergic rhinitis ★★★. Shankhaprakshalana: constipation. Trataka: convergence insufficiency, mental focus.Jala neti: ear pathology (perforation). Shankhaprakshalana: GI bleeding, hernia, recent abdominal surgery.

cards.title: Disease-Specific Asana Protocols
cards._legacyHtml:
ConditionRecommended AsanasPranayamaAvoid
HypertensionShavasana ★★★, Pawanmuktasana series, Tadasan, Sukhasana, Yoga Nidra. Slow forward bends (with support). Legs-up-the-wall (Viparita Karani).Nadi Shodhana ★★★ (alternate nostril), Bhramari ★★★, Chandra Bhedi (left nostril), Ujjayi (slow)Inversions (Shirshasana, Sarvangasana), Kapalbhati (aggressive), Bhastrika. Any vigorous vinyasa initially.
T2 DiabetesSurya Namaskar ★★★ (12 rounds, moderate pace), Mandukasana ★★ (stimulates pancreas), Ardha Matsyendrasana (spinal twist — compresses pancreatic region), Dhanurasana, NavasanaKapalbhati ★★★ (20–30 min — most evidence for DM), Bhastrika, Anulom-VilomAvoid in proliferative diabetic retinopathy (Kapalbhati raises intraocular pressure). Footwear for all standing poses (neuropathy — loss of proprioception).
Chronic LBPCat-cow (Marjaryasana-Bitilasana) ★★★, Child's pose (Balasana), Supine twist, Setu Bandha (bridge), Ardha Shalabhasana, Tadasana (alignment), PawanmuktasanaUjjayi during movement (reduces pain perception). Nadi Shodhana for pain anxiety.Deep forward folds (disc herniation), Halasana, unsupported Sarvangasana. No Bhujangasana (cobra) in acute phase.
Anxiety / DepressionSun salutations (energising for depression), Balasana (calming), Yoga Nidra ★★★, Viparita Karani (legs up wall), Savasana. Grounding poses: Tadasana, Vrksasana (tree).Bhramari ★★★ (vagus activation, anxiety ↓), Nadi Shodhana, 4-7-8 breathing. Ujjayi for depression (uplifting).Avoid vigorous practice in severe anxiety (activates SNS initially). Trauma history: trauma-sensitive yoga only — no hands-on adjustments without consent.
Asthma / COPDSukhasana with focus on exhalation, Bhujangasana (chest opener), Matsyasana (fish — opens chest), Gomukhasana (arms — stretches intercostals). Pursed lip breathing technique.Pursed lip breathing ★★★ (COPD — prevents dynamic airway collapse). Buteyko method ★★ (reduces hyperventilation). AVOID: Kapalbhati (may trigger bronchospasm). Ujjayi (slow).Bhastrika and Kapalbhati in active asthma. Any breath retention in acute phase. Inverted poses if severe dyspnoea.
PCOSBaddha Konasana ★★★ (butterfly — opens pelvis, ovarian stimulation), Setu Bandha, Balasana, Supta Baddha Konasana, Malasana (garland), Bharadvajasana (twist — liver detox)Nadi Shodhana (hormonal balance), Bhramari (reduces cortisol → ↓ androgens), Kapalbhati (metabolic)Avoid: vigorous practice during menstrual pain. Inversions during menstruation (traditional contraindication — patient choice).

cards.title: Shatkarmas (Yogic Purification Practices)
cards._legacyHtml:
Shatkarmas — The 6 Classical Cleansing Practices
Shatkarmas are mentioned in the Hatha Yoga Pradipika (15th century) as preparatory practices to purify the body before pranayama. They cleanse specific body channels (nadis) and organ systems. Clinically, several have strong modern evidence. All require proper instruction before independent practice.
ShatkarmaTechniqueIndicationContraindication
Jala Neti (Nasal irrigation)Saline solution (1 tsp salt/500ml lukewarm water) through neti pot, one nostril to other. Sutra neti: rubber catheter through nose, pulled out through mouth.Chronic sinusitis ★★★, allergic rhinitis ★★★, recurrent URTI, snoring, headache. Daily preventive.Ear infection, perforated eardrum, nasal polyps (relative), recent nasal surgery.
Kapalbhati (Frontal brain cleansing breathing)Rapid forceful exhalations (1–2/sec), passive inhalation. 1–3 rounds of 30–120 breaths. Generates heat, stimulates abdominal organs.T2DM ★★★ (most evidence), obesity, constipation, NAFLD, depression, brain fogHypertension (moderate-severe), active hernia, pregnancy, epilepsy, retinal detachment, recent abdominal surgery, acute cardiac disease.
Trataka (Concentrated gazing)Fixed gaze at a steady object (candle flame — bahir trataka, or point between eyebrows — antar trataka) without blinking until tears flow. 5–30 min.Convergence insufficiency, visual strain, concentration disorders, insomnia (activates parasympathetic), memory improvement.Glaucoma (may raise IOP). Dry eye disease. Epilepsy (flickering candle — use steady object instead).
Nauli (Abdominal isolation)Isolated contraction of rectus abdominis after exhalation. Advanced: churning motion. Massages digestive organs via mechanical compression.Constipation, digestive disorders, strengthens core, reproductive organ stimulation.Pregnancy, menstruation (traditional), hernia, recent abdominal surgery, any acute GI pathology.
Dhauti (Esophageal cleansing)Vaman dhauti: drink 4–6 glasses saline water on empty stomach, then induce vomiting by tickling back of throat. Clears excess kapha from stomach.Hyperacidity (paradoxical — removes acid burden), skin disorders (Ayurvedic rationale), obesity, kapha imbalance.Peptic ulcer, oesophageal varices, severe reflux, cardiac disease (vagal response risk), eating disorders (absolute contraindication — may reinforce purging behaviour).
Basti (Colon cleansing)Traditional: draw water into colon via rectal route. Modern clinical equivalent: therapeutic enema. Jala basti in water.Constipation, colonoscopy preparation, vata disorders, colon cleansing.Rectal bleeding, inflammatory bowel disease in flare, recent colorectal surgery, electrolyte imbalances.

cards.title: Yoga Nidra — Clinical Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Yoga Nidra — The Most Evidence-Based Yogic Relaxation Technique
Yoga Nidra (yogic sleep) is a systematic guided relaxation technique that induces a state between wakefulness and sleep (hypnagogic state), characterised by theta brainwave dominance. 1 hour of Yoga Nidra = 4 hours of restorative sleep (traditional claim). Research: dopamine release ↑ 65% (PET scan study), cortisol ↓ significantly, profound relaxation response, PTSD symptom reduction comparable to trauma-focused CBT in veterans.
1
Setting Intention (Sankalpa) Before induction, patient sets a short positive resolution (Sankalpa) — planted in the mind at the threshold of sleep when the subconscious is most receptive. Example: "I am healthy and at peace." "I am free from pain." Repeat 3 times mentally with feeling. Same sankalpa every session — cumulative neuroplastic effect over weeks of practice. This is the therapeutic foundation — not decoration.
2
Rotation of Consciousness Systematic rapid mental attention to each body part in specific sequence (right thumb → fingers → palm → back of hand → wrist... right side → left side → back → front → face → internal organs). Speed: 1 part per second approximately. Creates rapid alternating cortical arousal and inhibition → theta induction. Do not visualise — only feel/sense the part named. This is the core technique — practitioner reads sequence to patient.
3
Pairs of Opposites Patient experiences alternating opposite sensations: heaviness → lightness, heat → cold, pain → pleasure, sadness → joy. Each held for 5–10 seconds. Purpose: creates emotional detachment — the capacity to experience strong sensations without being overwhelmed by them. Particularly therapeutic for: chronic pain (desensitisation), anxiety (arousal tolerance training), PTSD (exposure without flooding).
4
Visualisation (Rapid Imagery) Short sequences of vivid images presented rapidly (1–2 sec each). Examples: "golden sun... a mountain lake... a rose... a red triangle..." The rapid imagery keeps the conscious mind engaged while the deeper mind remains in theta. Personalised visualisation for specific conditions: calming natural scenes for anxiety; healing light imagery for chronic illness; confidence and strength imagery for depression.
🟡 iRest — Trauma-Sensitive Yoga Nidra
iRest (Integrative Restoration) is a research-backed modification of Yoga Nidra for trauma, PTSD, chronic pain, and veterans. Key adaptations: no forced body scanning (patient chooses their level of engagement), "resource" state established first (safe place imagery), welcoming difficult sensations rather than pushing through them, always returning to resource if overwhelmed. iRest has US military hospital endorsement and multiple RCTs for PTSD, chronic pain, and TBI. Training available through iRest Institute. All BNYS practitioners working with trauma should use trauma-informed principles in yoga nidra guidance.
